title: Raynmaker is a ‘K-Pop crime thriller’ micro-drama made with AI - Music Ally
text:
We recently wrote about
how ‘micro-dramas’ have become big business in China
: $7bn a year being spent watching vertically-shot films split into sub-one-minute clips. Micro-dramas are catching on in the west too thanks to apps like ReelShort and DramaBox.
Now we’ve caught wind of an interesting music-focused example of the genre. Raynmaker is the work of London-based firm
Metrotone
. It’s an animated “K-Pop crime thriiller” that the company describes as “Human scripted. Human acted. 100% AI production and music”.
Co-founder Katharina Gellein Viken
gave more details
at the TellyCast Digital Content Forum conference in London yesterday. “We started by making music videos and then this micro-drama pilot. That’s tested really well with the audience, and now we have some big partners on board, we’re going to go with 22 episodes in spring,” she said.
You can
watch that first music video here
. “I’m a big believer in hybrid content, I don’t have a lot of faith in people wanting to watch all-AI content,” stressed Viken.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
